name: Airgap Networks (Acquired by Zscaler)
overview:

Specific Product / Service Types: Agentless network segmentation software, cloud-managed access control platform, device discovery and visibility tools, ransomware containment features, east-west traffic enforcement capabilities

Economic Buyer: CISO, CIO, VP Security
Department Budget Owner: Information Security / Network Security
Target Company Size: Mid-market to enterprise organizations
Target Industries: Technology, manufacturing, healthcare, financial services, energy, and other sectors operating critical or distributed network environments
Sales Motion: Enterprise cybersecurity direct sales supported by channel partners and Zero Trust ecosystem alliances
Replaces / Displaces: Network Access Control (NAC) solutions, traditional microsegmentation tools, east-west firewall architectures, manual network isolation processes
Named Competitors (if mentioned): Not always explicitly stated
Positioning Summary: Airgap Networks positioned itself as a provider of agentless Zero Trust segmentation technology designed to help organizations secure internal network traffic, prevent ransomware propagation, and modernize network security architectures without operational disruption.
primary_offering: Zero Trust network segmentation platform
